task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Synthetic data source is selected for the session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data source dropdown or active session indicator shows 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)'.

task_completion.description: The OpenBCI session is actively running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI shows active data streaming, indicated by a 'Stop Data Stream' button or moving waveforms in the interface.

task_completion.description: The Spectrogram widget is open in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel titled 'Spectrogram' is visible, displaying a 2D time-frequency heatmap with a color gradient.

task_completion.description: The Spectrogram widget is configured to display Channel 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the Spectrogram widget interface, the channel selection indicator or button is set to '2'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application is running natively with its standard interface, not simulated via a terminal script or text output.

integrity.description: The Spectrogram is rendered by the application, not an image viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Spectrogram is embedded inside the OpenBCI GUI layout, not opened in an image viewer, web browser, or paint application.

integrity.description: Agent did not just type the expected state into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors open with text claiming the task is complete (e.g., 'Spectrogram Channel 2 Synthetic').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI GUI data source dropdown for synthetic data is labeled 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)', despite the task description calling it 'Synthetic (PC)'. The Spectrogram widget displays a 2D time-frequency heatmap with a color gradient. The channel selection button for the Spectrogram widget is labeled '2' (not 'Channel 2').
